extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won from a 4-rider breakaway after personally driving the selection on Moeren straight, distancing Ewan/Bennett/Groenewegen despite doing huge workload.
tactical_patterns: Alpecin-Deceuninck pre-emptively attacked on Moeren straight despite having sprinter Philipsen, paradoxically distancing rival sprinters through overwork.
Soudal-QuickStep's defensive pace with 4 riders backfired, allowing Philipsen/Kooij breakaway while dropping own sprinter Groenewegen.
FRISON Frederik's monster gear bridge to 4-man group (solo) proved possible when rivals hesitated behind organized chase.
Punctures and mechanical attrition disproportionately affected second group chasers, suggesting front positioning trumps saving energy in wind-affected finales.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: Sustains winning speed after massive individual workload creating selection, unlike traditional sprinter energy management
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Dropped when Ballerini paced for Soudal-QuickStep despite team having 4-rider numerical advantage
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Distanced early on Moeren straight when Philipsen attacked, suggesting insufficient positioning or weakness in medium crosswinds
rider_signals.FRISON Frederik: positive: Solo bridged major gap pushing monster gear, finishing 4th after bridge work typically too costly
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Dropped from lead group after pacing work, suggesting poor form despite doing domestique duty
rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: neutral: Made 4-rider selection but Soudal-QuickStep's numerical advantage failed to control outcome against motivated Philipsen
